news.headline: Inspira Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd. prepares to ramp up INSPIRA™ ART100 production in response to China's respiratory virus.
news.summary: In Ra'anana, Israel, on January 10, 2025, Inspira Technologies OXY B.H.N. Ltd. revealed plans for accelerating production of its INSPIRA™ ART100 system. Inspira, known for pioneering life-support technology, is traded on Nasdaq under tickers IINN and IINNW. The company is gearing up for increased production to meet rising demand for its innovative product.

news.headline: Centrica warns UK gas reserves are concerningly low after freeze.
Fed unlikely to cut rates despite weak jobs report. Officials say more evidence needed to show labor market decline.
news.summary: Officials from the Federal Reserve have indicated that they will need to see more than one unfavorable report in order to be convinced that the labor market is declining.

Treasury market, facing difficulties, awaits jobs report for relief.
PacBio and Intus Bio partner to launch GutID, first at-home microbiome test, using Titan-1 platform & HiFi Sequencing, superior accuracy.
news.summary: In Menlo Park, California on January 10, 2025, PacBio and Intus Bio introduced GutID, the inaugural human gut health test. This test, powered by Intus Bio’s Titan-1 platform and PacBio’s HiFi sequencing technology, marks a significant advancement in microbiome research. Integrating Intus Bio’s strain-level resolution assay and AI-driven analysis with PacBio’s sequencing precision, GutID establishes a new standard in gut microbiome testing. This test stands out as the sole comprehensive and precise gut microbiome test currently on the market.

news.headline: Diamyd Medical reports progress towards Accelerated Approval for Diamyd®, finalizing key details after positive FDA meeting.
news.summary: In Stockholm on January 10, 2025, Diamyd Medical released an update regarding a successful Type C meeting with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) held in December 2024. The meeting, as per the final minutes received, concentrated on enhancing the study protocol and analysis.

news.headline: Important press releases, including highlights from CES 2025, in 16 newsworthy stories. NEW YORK, Jan. 10, 2025.
news.summary: Recent press releases from CES 2025 provide vital information. On January 10, 2025, in New York, over thousands of press releases were issued, making it challenging to stay updated. These releases are essential for journalists and consumers to remain informed about the most significant news of the week.

news.headline: Corus Entertainment reports Q1 results: 12% revenue decrease, 30% segment profit decrease, and $11.9 million net income.
news.summary: Corus Entertainment Inc. reported a 12% decrease in consolidated revenue and a 30% decrease in consolidated segment profit for the quarter ending November 30, 2024. The net income attributable to shareholders was $11.9 million, with a negative free cash flow of $10.1 million. The company launched two new lifestyle channel brands, Flavour Network and Home Network, and announced its winter 2025 lineup. Despite the decrease in financial metrics, the company remains focused on cost reductions and strengthening its balance sheet. Additionally, Corus saw a 24% increase in total hours streamed across streaming platforms and an increase in overall share of viewing. The company expects further declines in advertising revenue in the second quarter of fiscal 2025 but plans to implement additional cost reduction initiatives to mitigate these challenges. Management cautions that the operating performance of the company may be affected by economic conditions and industry risks.

news.headline: Mersana Therapeutics receives FDA Fast Track Designation for Emiltatug Ledadotin (XMT-1660), with conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET to review Phase 1 clinical data.
news.summary: A conference call is scheduled for today at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time to review the encouraging preliminary results from Phase 1 clinical trials. The call will focus on discussing the positive initial data from the trial.

Maple Leaf Foods prepares for transformational year ahead in 2025 with restructuring phase completed. TSX: MFI.
news.summary: Maple Leaf Foods Inc., a renowned Canadian protein company listed on the TSX under the ticker symbol MFI, released details about its 2025 outlook and the successful conclusion of the first phase of restructuring. The company, headquartered in Mississauga, Ontario, disclosed this information on January 10, 2025. Investors can find further information on Maple Leaf Foods by visiting www.mapleleaffoods.com.

news.headline: Biodesix's unaudited 2024 results show revenue growth of 45% to $71.3 million for the full year.
news.summary: Reported revenues for the full year reached around $71.3 million, marking a substantial growth of 45% compared to the previous year.

news.headline: Armada Hoffler's outlook upgraded to stable by Morningstar, BBB credit rating confirmed.
news.summary: Virginia Beach, Virginia on January 10, 2025: Armada Hoffler (NYSE: AHH) disclosed that Morningstar has raised its forecast to stable and reaffirmed its BBB credit rating.

news.headline: Aura Minerals Inc. achieved 66,473 gold equivalent ounces in Q4 2024, meeting production guidance. Almas had an 11% increase.
news.summary: Aura Minerals Inc., a mining company, recently disclosed its preliminary production figures for Q4 2024 from its active mines in Aranzazu, Apoena (EPP), Minosa (San Andres), and Almas. The total production for Q4 2024 was 66,473 gold equivalent ounces ("GEO"), a slight decrease of 1% from the previous quarter but consistent with the same period last year at consistent metal prices. Noteworthy highlights from the quarter include Almas, which achieved a production record with an 11% increase from the previous quarter, surpassing annual guidance. Additionally, Minosa exceeded its annual guidance targets during this period.

news.headline: Mersana Therapeutics reports positive clinical data for Emiltatug Ledadotin (XMT-1660) in Phase 1 trial, initiating expansion in TNBC. Exciting responses seen in patients with TNBC, with confirmed responses across enrolled tumor types. Expansion cohort started in TNBC patients previously treated with topo-1 ADCs, with dose exploration in progress. Expected 2025 milestones and focus areas outlined. Conference call at 8:30 a.m. ET.
news.summary: Emiltatug ledadotin shows favorable tolerability with a distinct safety profile. Encouraging clinical outcomes are evident in patients with triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) previously exposed to topoisomerase-1 inhibitor ADCs, with positive responses seen in all tumor types studied. A new cohort targeting TNBC patients who have undergone at least one topo-1 ADC treatment has been initiated, and dosage investigations are ongoing. The company has disclosed anticipated milestones and key areas of attention through 2025. A conference call will be held today at 8:30 a.m. ET.

news.headline: Li-Cycle gives preliminary 2024 highlights, gaining traction as lithium-ion recycling partner with top global customers.
news.summary: In Toronto, Li-Cycle Holdings Corp. (NYSE: LICY) announced its preliminary commercial and operational achievements for the full year of 2024. The company, a prominent global lithium-ion battery resource recovery firm, experienced significant growth in commercial success throughout the year. Li-Cycle enhanced its reputation as a top choice for lithium-ion battery recycling partnerships, particularly with major clients in the battery supply chain industry, such as EV OEMs. Through these collaborations, the company solidified its position in the market.

news.headline: ChipMOS predicts 6.3% full-year 2024 revenue growth; December 2024 revenue down 5.4% YoY; 4Q24 revenue decline 5.7% YoY.
news.summary: On January 10, 2025, in Hsinchu, ChipMOS TECHNOLOGIES INC. announced its unaudited consolidated financial results. ChipMOS is a prominent provider of outsourced semiconductor assembly and test services. The company trades on the Taiwan Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol 8150 and on Nasdaq as IMOS.

news.headline: Ouro Medicines secures $120M to develop T cell engagers for chronic immune diseases, promising clinical-stage asset.
news.summary: A company called Ouro has secured a substantial $120 million in funding to focus on creating innovative T cell engagers aimed at resetting the immune systems of individuals suffering from chronic immune-mediated ailments. The company is kicking off with an encouraging clinical-stage product and a strong pipeline for further discoveries. Monograph Capital played a key role in the establishment of Ouro.

news.headline: GeForce NOW widens cloud gaming at CES 2025, adds new titles and partners like Steam Deck, Apple Vision Pro.
news.summary: At CES 2025, NVIDIA reveals plans to broaden GeForce NOW cloud gaming by adding new games and collaborating with popular platforms such as Steam Deck and Apple Vision Pro. This expansion is set to offer gamers access to an even wider variety of titles and opportunities, consolidating NVIDIA's position as a leading player in the cloud gaming market.

news.headline: Everything Blockchain Inc. (OTCMKTS: EBZT) resolves all toxic convertible notes previously held by 1800 Diagonal Lending, LLC.
news.summary: On January 10, 2025, Everything Blockchain Inc. (OTCMKTS: EBZT) revealed that they have effectively resolved all harmful convertible notes that were formerly owned by 1800 Diagonal Lending, LLC. This move signifies the company's commitment to connecting established markets with new technologies like artificial intelligence and blockchain.

news.headline: Shareholders of Customers Bancorp (NYSE: CUBI) urged to contact Levi & Korsinsky by Jan 31, 2025 regarding class action lawsuit.
news.summary: On January 10, 2025, Levi & Korsinsky, LLP announced a class action securities lawsuit aimed at recovering losses for investors in Customers Bancorp, Inc. (NYSE: CUBI). The lawsuit is specifically targeting those who have suffered losses due to alleged wrongdoings by Customers Bancorp, Inc. The class definition includes all individuals who were shareholders of the company during the period in question. Levi & Korsinsky, LLP is leading the legal action on behalf of these investors to seek compensation for their losses.

news.headline: Ex-official involved in bribery case in Russia had $10M in Bitcoin seized; funds to be added to state revenue.
news.summary: The Russian state will convert the confiscated Bitcoin, which was taken from Marat Tambiev, a former ICRF staff member, into state revenue. The funds were held in a hardware crypto wallet.

news.headline: Contact Levi & Korsinsky before Jan 21, 2025 to discuss Xerox Holdings Corp. securities lawsuit (NASDAQ: XRX). Loss recovery for investors.
news.summary: On January 10, 2025, Levi & Korsinsky, LLP issued a notification to investors in Xerox Holdings Corporation (NASDAQ: XRX) regarding a class action securities lawsuit. The lawsuit aims to recuperate losses on behalf of Xerox shareholders. The legal action concerns allegations against Xerox Holdings Corporation and is open to investors who purchased shares between specific dates. This class action lawsuit highlights the importance of investor rights protection and seeks to address any potential damages suffered by shareholders.

news.headline: Becrypt released VDI Guard to protect critical infrastructure from remote access protocol vulnerabilities.
news.summary: In London, Becrypt, a prominent cybersecurity company, has unveiled a new offering called Becrypt VDI Guard. This innovative solution is aimed at addressing the growing concerns related to security vulnerabilities in remote access protocols. With cyber threats evolving rapidly, protecting critical national infrastructure (CNI) has become a top priority. Remote access protocols like RDP, SSH, VDI, and VNC play a crucial role in modern IT infrastructure. Becrypt VDI Guard is designed to enhance security measures in this area.

GlobalLogic appoints Srini Shankar as President and CEO, starting February 3, 2025. Nitesh Banga steps down after joining in 2018.
news.summary: In Santa Clara, California, GlobalLogic Inc., part of the Hitachi Group and a top player in Digital Engineering, declared that Srinivas (Srini) Shankar, the Chief Business Officer and Global Industries Head, will become the new President and CEO starting February 3, 2025. Srini is taking over from Nitesh Banga, who opted to resign in order to explore fresh avenues beyond Hitachi and GlobalLogic. Nitesh had joined GlobalLogic in June 2018 and made significant contributions during his tenure.

news.headline: AAC Technologies presented advanced sensory solutions at CES 2025, enhancing digital experiences through visual, audio, and haptic elements. #innovation #CES2025
news.summary: In Las Vegas on January 10, 2025, AAC Technologies unveiled innovative advancements in sensory interaction at CES 2025, the prominent consumer electronics and technology trade show. The exhibition highlighted cutting-edge experiences encompassing visual, audio, and haptic technologies. AAC Technologies demonstrated pioneering solutions aimed at enhancing sensory engagement in the digital realm, captivating audiences at the event.

news.summary: According to the platform's CEO, certain AI agents developed by Oh are already yielding more than $10,000 in monthly revenue.

Supreme Court to review a law threatening to close TikTok, balancing national security with free speech.
news.summary: The judges are anticipated to make a prompt decision in the lawsuit, where national security worries regarding China clash with the First Amendment’s safeguarding of free speech.

news.headline: Impact of Northern Lights and digital photography on astrotourism, leading to surge in visitors by 20% last year.
China's central bank halts bond purchases amid deflation worries, aiming to curb bond market bubble caused by risk aversion.
news.summary: The surprising move aims to prevent a possible bubble in the bond market due to investors avoiding riskier assets such as stocks and real estate.

news.headline: Economists' influence wanes due to flawed forecasts and jargon. Can they regain credibility in shaping policy?
news.summary: For many years, economists have played a crucial role in influencing policy decisions regarding taxes and health care. However, their credibility has been undermined due to inaccurate predictions and the use of complex language. This has led to a lack of faith in their forecasts and recommendations. As a result, the public may be less likely to trust economic experts when it comes to critical issues such as fiscal policies and healthcare reforms. This highlights the importance of clear communication and accurate analysis in order to regain the trust of the public.

news.headline: FTSE 100 Update: Gilt yields up for 5th day, Pound at around $1.23.
news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is expected to grow by 5.6% in 2021, rebounding from a -3.5% contraction in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Advanced economies are projected to grow by 5.4%, while emerging market and developing economies are expected to grow by 6%. In comparison, China's economy is forecasted to grow by 8.4% in 2021. This positive outlook is largely attributed to the rollout of vaccines, fiscal stimulus measures, and improving business and consumer confidence. However, there are concerns about uneven vaccine distribution and the emergence of new virus variants that could pose risks to the economic recovery.

news.headline: EBF: Three-month Euribor falls 2.3 BPS.
Trump has picked 8 economic advisors to support tariffs and tax cuts in his administration.
news.summary: The elected President, Donald J. Trump, has shifted gears from his initial term's team-of-rivals strategy. His selection of economic advisors reflects a focus on supporting tariffs and tax reductions.

news.headline: China Telecom's TeleNavi introduces voice-only intelligent location technology to help elderly overcome digital travel dilemma. (Characters: 111)
news.summary: In Hong Kong on January 10, 2025, a report highlighted the challenges faced by elderly individuals in adapting to digital technology advancements. Termed as the 'Digital Travel Dilemma,' the swift evolution of the internet has notably improved convenience for the younger demographic. However, the elderly population is encountering difficulties in keeping up with smartphone usage. This issue has become more prevalent as services such as e-map continue to grow in popularity.

news.headline: UK economic project under threat as bond selloff intensifies, with Reeves facing crucial turning point.
CoreWeave appoints Sandy Venugopal as new CIO to lead digital transformation, boosting innovation and growth efforts.
news.summary: CoreWeave has announced the appointment of Sandy Venugopal as its new Chief Information Officer. Venugopal will lead the company's IT and digital transformation efforts, focusing on driving innovation and fostering growth. This move comes as CoreWeave aims to strengthen its position in the market and capitalize on emerging opportunities. Venugopal's expertise in IT and technology will be instrumental in propelling the company forward. We are excited to see the impact of this strategic appointment on CoreWeave's future endeavors.

news.headline: Bybit & Block Scholes analyze BTC drop below $100k as market awaits "Crypto President" inauguration.
news.summary: On January 10, 2025, in Dubai, UAE, Bybit, the second-largest cryptocurrency exchange based on trading volume globally, unveiled its most recent crypto derivatives report in collaboration with Blocks Scholes. The report, issued weekly, highlighted Bitcoin's decrease from $100,000 within the first week of the new year.
